merger_description:
The application concerns the proposed acquisition of sole control over FDH Defense Aftermarket, Inc. and its subsidiary, and FDH Group Intermediate, Inc. and its direct and indirect subsidiaries (collectively, **FDH**), by funds managed and/or advised by Bain Capital Investors, LLC (**Bain Capital**). Pursuant to a Stock Purchase Agreement, BCPE Lightning Buyer, Inc, a special purpose vehicle owned and controlled by funds managed and/or advised by Bain Capital, will acquire 100% of the shares in FDH from FDH Group Intermediate Holdings, LP.

Bain Capital is a private investment firm that invests, through its family of funds, in companies across a number of industries, including information technology, healthcare, retail and consumer products, communications, financial services and industrial/manufacturing.

FDH is a global supply chain partner and distributor of aerospace and defence components, specializing in hardware, electrical, and consumable products. It is headquartered in California, and supplies original equipment manufacturer and aftermarket customers around the world with inventory management and logistics. FDH operates across three business segments:

* FDH Hardware (distributing fasteners, fittings, bearings, and other hardware),
* FDH Electronics (distributing interconnect and electronic components, cable assemblies, and custom wire harnesses), and
* FDH Defense Aftermarket (distributing aftermarket components).

FDH is ultimately controlled by Audax Management Company, LLC, a private equity platform headquartered in Boston that focuses on investing across the North American middle market.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BCPE Lightning Buyer, Inc (Acquirer), a special purpose vehicle
owned and controlled by funds managed and/or advised by Bain
Capital Investors, LLC (Bain Capital), applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the shares in FDH
Defense Aftermarket, Inc. and its subsidiary, and FDH Group
Intermediate, Inc. and its direct and indirect subsidiaries (together,
FDH), as described in the transaction documents provided as part of
the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, Bain Capital, is a private investment firm that invests,
through its family of funds, in companies across a number of
industries, including information technology, healthcare, retail and
consumer products, communications, financial services, and
industrial and manufacturing.
The target, FDH, is a global supply chain partner and distributor of
aerospace and defence components, specialising in hardware,
electrical, and consumable products. It is headquartered in California
and supplies original equipment manufacturer and aftermarket
customers around the world with inventory management and
logistics. FDH operates across 3 business segments:
a. FDH Hardware: distributing fasteners, fittings, bearings, and
other hardware
b. FDH Electronics: distributing interconnect and electronic
components, cable assemblies, and custom wire harnesses
c. FDH Defense Aftermarket: distributing aftermarket
components.
FDH is ultimately controlled by Audax Management Company, LLC, a
private equity platform headquartered in Boston that focuses on
investing across the North American middle market.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the parties or
their connected entities in Australia
b. while there is a vertical supply relationship between
FDH and certain connected entities of Bain Capital,
there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other
concerning vertical effects arising from the
Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
